After d/t/n, the spelling stays -dńõ/-tńõ/-nńõ (only the pronunciation softens).

whyNotes.gen_sg: Soudce-type nouns take -e in genitive singular (e.g. soudce).
whyNotes.dat_sg: Soudce-type nouns take -i in dative singular (e.g. soudci).
whyNotes.acc_sg: Soudce-type nouns take -e in accusative singular (e.g. soudce). For animate masculine nouns in the singular, accusative is the same as genitive. (In the plural, accusative is -e but genitive is -ŇĮ.)
whyNotes.voc_sg: Soudce-type nouns keep -e in vocative singular (e.g. soudce). Same as nominative.
whyNotes.loc_sg: Soudce-type nouns take -i in locative singular (e.g. soudci). Same ending as dative.
whyNotes.ins_sg: Soudce-type nouns take -em in instrumental singular (e.g. soudcem).
whyNotes.nom_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -i or -ov√© in nominative plural (e.g. soudci, soudcov√©).
whyNotes.gen_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -ŇĮ in genitive plural (e.g. soudcŇĮ).
whyNotes.dat_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -ŇĮm in dative plural (e.g. soudcŇĮm).
whyNotes.acc_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -e in accusative plural (e.g. soudce).
whyNotes.voc_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -i or -ov√© in vocative plural. Vocative plural is always the same as nominative plural.
whyNotes.loc_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -√≠ch in locative plural (e.g. soudc√≠ch).
whyNotes.ins_pl: Soudce-type nouns take -i in instrumental plural (e.g. soudci).
